metadata.summary: Complete reference for Claude Messages API: request structure, parameters, response fields, multi-turn history, vision (images/PDFs), and streaming patterns.

content.title: Claude Messages API
content.summary: The Claude Messages API is the single completion endpoint for all Claude calls. Covers basic request structure, parameters, response object, multi-turn history management, vision (base64 and URL, images and PDFs), streaming (text_stream and event-based), and SSE event format. The core rule: append the full `resp.content` list (not just text) as the assistant turn in multi-turn history — tool_use blocks must be preserved or the next turn gets a 400 error.

why.p: The Messages API is the integration point for every Claude-based agent. Its `stop_reason` field drives agent branching logic: `"end_turn"` is normal, `"max_tokens"` is silent truncation, `"tool_use"` means the loop must continue. Without explicit `stop_reason` handling and correct multi-turn history construction, agents silently return truncated text, discard tool state, or break with consecutive same-role messages.

list.item: All direct Claude API calls — Messages is the only completion endpoint.
Streaming responses to a user interface or pipeline sink consuming partial text.
Multi-turn agent conversations with explicit message history management.
Vision tasks: screenshot analysis, document OCR, UI inspection, PDF summarization.
When the agent needs to detect `stop_reason` to branch logic.
When persistent thread storage is needed across sessions — build your own; Anthropic has no Threads equivalent.
When guaranteed schema compliance without retry loops is required — use OpenAI `beta.parse` or `instructor`.
High-volume batch jobs — prefer the Batch API (50% cost reduction).
Computer Use or Extended Thinking inside a stateless lambda — those require multi-turn conversation state.

section.name: basic-request
section.p: The Messages API takes model, max_tokens, system prompt, and message history.

example.title: Minimal API call
example.code:
import anthropic
client = anthropic.Anthropic()
message = client.messages.create( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, system="You are an expert on Specification-Driven Development. Be concise.", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What are the key phases of SDD?"}] ) print(message.content[0].text)

section.name: parameters
section.p: The key parameters that control the request behavior.

rule.statement: Always set max_tokens explicitly — silent truncation (stop_reason="max_tokens") is the most common cause of corrupted downstream data.
rule.rationale: max_tokens is required and easy to set too low; truncation produces parseable but incomplete output that passes format checks and fails semantic ones.

list.item: model: required — model ID string.
max_tokens: required — max response tokens; cannot be omitted; truncates if too low.
messages: required — alternating user/assistant turns; consecutive same-role messages → 400 error.
system: optional — set behavior; kept stable across calls for Prompt Caching benefit.
temperature: optional — 0–1 (not 0–2 like OpenAI); 0=deterministic, 1=creative.
stop_sequences: optional — matches on next token boundary; mid-sentence stop is cut silently.
metadata: optional — add user_id for abuse detection and cost attribution.

section.name: response-structure-and-stop-reason
section.p: The response object contains content blocks, usage, and stop_reason. Stop_reason drives agent branching.

rule.statement: Check stop_reason before accessing content[0].text — if stop_reason is "tool_use", content[0] may be a tool_use block, not text.
rule.rationale: Content blocks are polymorphic; indexing blindly into content[0].text raises AttributeError when the first block is a tool_use or thinking block.

example.title: Inspecting response fields

section.name: multi-turn-pattern
section.p: Multi-turn conversations require careful history management to preserve tool_use blocks.

rule.statement: Append the full resp.content list (not just text) as the assistant turn in multi-turn history — tool_use blocks must be preserved or the next turn gets a 400 error.
rule.rationale: The API reconstructs conversation state from message history; missing tool_use blocks make the history inconsistent and cause an API error.

example.title: Multi-turn with full content preservation
example.code: messages = [ {"role": "user", "content": "What is SDD?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "SDD (Specification-Driven Development) is..."}, {"role": "user", "content": "How does it compare to TDD?"} ] response = client.messages.create(model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, messages=messages) # Append full content list, not just text messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response.content})

section.name: image-from-base64
section.p: Send local files by base64-encoding them.

rule.statement: Place image content before text content in multimodal messages — model processes images better when they come first in the content array.
rule.rationale: Empirically, image comprehension quality improves when the image precedes the question about it; this is consistent across Claude model versions.

example.title: Vision: base64-encoded screenshot

rule.statement: Image URLs are fetched at inference time — if the URL returns a 403 at that moment, the call fails silently with a content error block rather than an API error.
rule.rationale: URL-based images depend on the URL being accessible at inference time; pre-fetch and base64-encode for reliability in production pipelines.

example.title: Vision: URL-based image

section.name: pdf-documents
section.p: Send PDFs for document analysis and summarization.

rule.statement: Validate PDF page count before sending — PDFs exceeding 100 pages produce an API error, not a truncation; validate before sending to prevent pipeline failures.
rule.rationale: The error surfaces at inference time after the full PDF has been base64-encoded and transmitted; validating first avoids wasting the encoding and transmission.

example.title: Vision: PDF document

list.item: JPEG / PNG / GIF / WebP: max 20MB each.
PDF: max 32MB / 100 pages.

section.name: text-stream-pattern
section.p: Simple streaming pattern for consuming text output incrementally.

example.title: Streaming with text_stream
example.code: with client.messages.stream( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a poem about AI"}] ) as stream: for text in stream.text_stream: print(text, end="", flush=True) # Get usage stats after streaming completes final = stream.get_final_message() print(f"\nTokens: {final.usage.input_tokens} in, {final.usage.output_tokens} out")
example.note: Use stream.get_final_message() when usage stats are needed — they are not available from individual chunks.

section.name: event-based-streaming
section.p: Fine-grained event-based streaming for tool use and custom control.

rule.statement: For streaming + tool use, accumulate input_json_delta events and parse after content_block_stop — never parse partial JSON inline.
rule.rationale: input_json_delta events carry fragments of JSON that are not individually parseable; parsing before the block stops raises JSONDecodeError.

example.title: Event-based streaming with tool detection

section.name: async-streaming
section.p: Asynchronous streaming for concurrent pipeline processing.

example.title: Async streaming with AsyncAnthropic
example.code:
import asyncio import anthropic
async_client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()
async def stream_response(): async with async_client.messages.stream( model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514", max_tokens=1024, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}] ) as stream: async for text in stream.text_stream: print(text, end="", flush=True)
asyncio.run(stream_response())

section.name: sse-event-format
section.p: Raw SSE format reference for custom transport implementations.

list.item: message_start: message metadata (id, model, usage placeholder).
content_block_start: new content block begins (type: text or tool_use).
content_block_delta: text_delta or input_json_delta.
content_block_stop: block complete.
message_delta: final usage stats.
message_stop: message complete.

rule.statement: Claude SSE format differs from OpenAI SSE — they are not interchangeable without an adapter layer (e.g., LiteLLM).
rule.rationale: OpenAI uses data: [DONE] to end a stream; Claude uses message_stop event. The delta object structure also differs; treating them as equivalent causes silent parse failures.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: The Messages API is the single integration point for all Claude calls in an agent system. A subagent constructs the message array (system + alternating user/assistant), calls `client.messages.create` or `client.messages.stream`, inspects `stop_reason`, and routes to the appropriate next step. For long-running pipelines, stream responses to a queue and process in a consumer. For multi-turn, append the assistant response to the message array before sending the next user turn.
section.note: Recommended subagents: faion-sdd-executor-agent (uses Messages API for all Claude calls; explicit stop_reason handling per phase), nero-sdd-executor-agent (multi-turn conversation with accumulated message history for context).

list.item: Always check `resp.stop_reason` before accessing `resp.content[0].text` — tool_use blocks are not text.
Append the full `resp.content` list (not just text) as the assistant turn in multi-turn history — tool_use blocks must be preserved.
Use `stream.get_final_message()` when you need usage stats from a streaming call.
Place image content before text content in multi-modal messages — model processes images better when they come first.
Use `metadata={"user_id": "..."}` to tag calls for abuse detection and cost attribution.
For PDF analysis, check page count first; split at 50 pages if near the 100-page limit.
Keep `system` prompt stable across calls in a session to enable Prompt Caching benefits.

section.name: ai-agent-gotchas
section.p: Common failure modes when integrating the Messages API into agent systems.

antipattern.description: `resp.content` is a `list[ContentBlock]` — content blocks can be `text`, `tool_use`, or `thinking`; always type-check before accessing `.text`.
antipattern.reason: Blind indexing into content[0].text fails when the first block is not text. Check the type first.

antipattern.description: Streaming with tools: `input_json_delta` events carry partial JSON — you must accumulate and parse after `content_block_stop`, not inline.
antipattern.reason: Partial JSON is not valid; parsing before the block finishes raises JSONDecodeError. Wait for content_block_stop before parsing.

antipattern.description: Multi-turn history must alternate user/assistant roles strictly — consecutive same-role messages raise a 400 error.
antipattern.reason: The API validates role alternation. Appending text instead of the full content block breaks this invariant.

antipattern.description: `stop_sequences` match on the next token boundary — if the model generates a stop sequence mid-sentence, the sentence is cut off without warning.
antipattern.reason: Stop sequences are a hard boundary, not a soft hint. Mid-generation stops lose data without notification.

antipattern.description: Image URLs in messages are fetched at inference time — if the URL returns a 403 at that moment, the call fails silently with a content error block.
antipattern.reason: URL-based access is async. Pre-fetch and base64-encode for reliability.

antipattern.description: PDF pages exceeding 100 produce an API error, not a truncation — validate before sending.
antipattern.reason: Over-limit PDFs fail after encoding and transmission. Validate page count first to avoid wasted bandwidth.

template.purpose: Multi-turn agent loop with explicit stop_reason branching and max-turns guard.
analyze_screenshot() helper using base64 encoding for local files.
